mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates expanding economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDP growth exceeds 3% annualized but 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of quarterly observations over a 5-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical threshold, it signals labor market tightness that historically accompanies sustained economic strength and rising equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after trigger activation 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts and industrial production reflect broad-based domestic demand and manufacturing strength, reinforcing each other to generate positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in months when both HOUST and INDPRO are above their 12-month moving averages, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 40% of cases over a 5-year period, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound where the federal funds rate is near zero, further accommodation is impossible and low rates signal pessimistic growth expectations, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index above a moderate threshold signals overheating and impending monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after trigger activation, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in months when RSAFS increases sequentially, 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over any 2-year window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate jointly signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: If when both conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below a key level signals contraction in the manufacturing sector, foreshadowing broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after trigger activation, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, reject hypothesis.
